overall.summary: Background and face sub-parts (eyes, nose, lips, eyebrows) are precise. Parent person layer is nearly empty because z-priority subtraction gave all pixels to sub-parts — this is design intent but counterintuitive. Chair_parapet bbox too wide, includes arms/hands that belong to person.

layers.name: mona_lisa
layers.issue: Parent person layer has <3% pixels after sub-parts (skin/hair/eyes/etc) claimed via z-priority. Visually shows only hair strands and arm fragments.
layers.fix: Either (a) suppress parent layer when expand_face_parts=true, or (b) add 'body_remainder' layer for torso/neck/clothing pixels not covered by face parts. Option (b) is more useful for compositing.

layers.name: mona_lisa__hair
layers.issue: Only top-of-head hair strand captured; missing long hair falling on shoulders
layers.fix: Face-parsing hair class misses shoulder-length hair below face bbox. Extend hair bbox vertically or fallback to DINO 'long dark hair falling on shoulders'.

layers.name: chair_parapet
layers.issue: Bbox too wide — captured the entire lower-body region including hands/arms instead of just chair armrest + parapet
layers.fix: Tighten bbox via a different DINO prompt like 'stone ledge only' excluding hands, or post-process: subtract person bbox area from chair_parapet mask

missing_concepts: dress/clothing (absorbed into parent 'mona_lisa' layer which is now empty)
veil (subtle translucent fabric, likely below detection threshold)
neck (partially in skin layer but not separately named)

recommended_reruns.action: Either drop the parent layer from manifest when expand_face_parts=true, OR add explicit 'clothing' and 'hands' sub-entities to the plan so body parts get their own layers.
Split into separate 'chair_armrest' and 'stone_parapet_ledge' with tighter DINO prompts; add negative prompt ignoring human arms.
Face-parsing misses below-chin hair. Supplement with DINO 'long dark brown hair falling below shoulders' as separate entity.
